data.Modification: Iodoacetamide derivative/Addition of Glycine/Addition of G

project.title: N1-Methylpseudouridine and pseudouridine modifications modulate mRNA decoding during translation
project.projectDescription: Mass spectrometry on affinity-purified luciferase produced from constitutively modified or unmodified mRNA in HEK293 cells.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: 293H cells were transfected with modified or unmodified luciferase mRNA and harvested 6 hours after transfection. HA-Luciferase-FLAG protein was purified by tandem affinity purification (FLAG then HA) and separated by SDS-PAGE. Silver-stained bands were excised, detstained, reduced with DTT and alkylated with iodoacetamide, then digested in-gel with trypsin. Peptides were eluted, lyophilized, and resuspended in acetonitrile with 0.1% TFA. Peptides were separated on a 25 cm Magic C18AQ column using a linear gradient of 5-35% acetonitrile + 0.1% formic acid against mobile phase A (water + 0.1% formic acid). Ions were introduced by positive electrospray ionization into a Thermo Scientific Q exactive hybrid mass spectrometer. Mass spectra were acquired over m/z 300-1750 at 70,000 resolution (m/z 200) with an AGC target of 1e6, and datadependent acquisition selected the top 10 most abundant precursor ions for tandem mass spectrometry by HCD fragmentation using an isolation width of 1.6 Da, max fill time of 110 ms, and AGC target of 1e5. Peptides were fragmented by a normalized collisional energy of 27, and fragment spectra acquired at a resolution of 17,500 (m/z 200).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw data files were peak picked by Proteome Discoverer version 2.1 and searched against SwissProt Human FASTA (5/2018) with the luciferase sequence added. Search parameters included Trypsin/P specificity, up to 2 missed cleavages, a fixed modification of carbamidomethyl cysteine, and variable modifications of oxidized methionine, pyroglutamic acid for Q, and N-terminal acetylation. The processed peak list was then re-searched in MASCOT against luciferase only, using an error-tolerant search allowing for all amino acid substitutions. High probability substitutions, as determined by greater than 90% peptide probability in Scaffold (version 4.8.8), were then added back to the original search parameters in Proteome Discoverer for interrogation by MASCOT as variable modifications for confirmation.

references.referenceLine: Monroe J, Eyler DE, Mitchell L, Deb I, Bojanowski A, Srinivas P, Dunham CM, Roy B, Frank AT, Koutmou KS. N1-Methylpseudouridine and pseudouridine modifications modulate mRNA decoding during translation. Nat Commun. 2024 15(1):8119

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic: Three independent transfections were performed for uridine/ N1-methylpseudouridine -containing mRNAs.

Comment[Separation]: UPLC was performed on a Waters NanoAcquity instrument
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: Data-dependent acquisition selected the ten most abundant precursor ions for HCD fragmentation using an isolation width of 1.6 Da, fill time of 110 ms, and an AGC target of 1e5.
